zh.Maxey, Northamptonshire,England: 英國英格蘭坎布利其爾馬可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de la Mare II於1265年出生在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾馬可時，他的父親Sir Peter de la Mare27歲，母親Christian De La Mere25歲。他於1284年1月之後與Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1343年在英國坎布利其爾馬可享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Mare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Americo, Cesareo, Fernando, Ines, Javier, Jose, Marcos.  Portuguese Henrique.  Italian Antonio, Gino, Olindo, Silvio, Vito.  Dutch Andries.
Italian: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mare ‘sea’.
English: variant of   Mear  .

variants.name: Marucci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Antonio, Silvio, Angelo, Carmela, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Guido, Licinio, Lorenzo, Luciano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Maruccio, a pet form of Mare or Mari .

variants.name: Marelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Giuseppe, Angelo, Dante.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Mare  .

variants.name: Mear
meanings: English: variant of Mear , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: from Ó Meidhir ‘descendant of Meidhir’, a personal name based on meidhir ‘mirth’. Compare Myers .

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de la Mare II was born in 1265, in Maxey, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Peter de la Mare, was 27 and his mother, Christian De La Mere, was 25. He married Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham after January 1284. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1343, in Maxey, Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de la Mare II est né en 1265, à Maxey, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Peter de la Mare, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Christian De La Mere, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham après janvier 1284. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1343, à Maxey, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de la Mare II 1265 in Maxey, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Peter de la Mare 27 und seine Mutter Christian De La Mere 25. Er hat nach Januar 1284 Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1343 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Maxey, Cambridgeshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Geoffrey de la Mare II è nato il nel 1265 nel Maxey, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Peter de la Mare, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Christian De La Mere, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham dopo il gennaio 1284. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1343, nel Maxey, Cambridgeshire, England, all'età di 78 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de la Mare II(이)가 1265년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 맥세이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Peter de la Mare(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Christian De La Mere(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1284년 1월 이후 Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1343년 영국 캄브리지실르 맥세이에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de la Mare II родился в 1265 г. в местности макси, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Peter de la Mare было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Christian De La Mere, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham после январь 1284 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1343 г. в местности макси, камбриджешир, Англия в возрасте 78 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Geoffrey de la Mare II nació en 1265, en Maxey, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Peter de la Mare, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Christian De La Mere, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Mrs. Geoffrey Greenham después de enero de 1284. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1343, en Maxey, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 78 años.
